content.problemStatement:
During Eid, it's a tradition that each father gives his kids money which they can spend on entertainment.

Chef has NNN coins; let's denote the value of coin iii by viv_ivi‚Äã. Since today is Eid, Chef is going to give one coin to each of his two children. He wants the absolute value of the difference between the values of coins given to the two children to be as small as possible, so that he would be as fair as possible.

Help Chef by telling him the minimum possible difference between the values of the coins given to the two children. Of course, Chef cannot give the same coin to both children.
constraints: the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 2,0002,0002,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vNv_1, v_2, \dots, v_Nv1‚Äã,v2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,vN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the minimum possible difference.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef gives the coin with value 111 to his first child and the coin with value 222 to the second child, so the answer is 2‚àí1=12-1 = 12‚àí1=1.
Example case 2: Chef gives each of his children a coin with value 333, so the difference is 000.
